title: gratiola ramosa subsp. drummondii
intro: Gratiola ramosa subsp. drummondii, also known as Drummond's blue-cup, is a plant species within the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly found in North America. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The health benefits of Gratiola ramosa subsp. drummondii include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and alleviate congestion. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a safe and effective manner.
